name: hugo-blog-post
description: Write a Technical Blog Post for Personal Website
---
# Skill: Write a Technical Blog Post for Personal Website (Conversational, Journalistic Style)

## Purpose
Guide yourself through writing a technically deep, engaging blog post for your Hugo-based personal website, showcasing data science and machine learning work. The workflow emphasizes a journalistic, story-driven approach, technical depth, and Hugo-specific publishing steps.

---

## Workflow

### 1. Define the Core Storyline
- Brainstorm a central narrative or hook (problem, surprise, challenge, or discovery).
- Outline the journey: how you encountered the problem, what made it interesting, and why it matters.
- Decide on the emotional arc (curiosity, frustration, breakthrough, etc.).

### 2. Plan the Structure (Journalistic, Not Scientific)
- Sketch a rough outline:
  - Catchy introduction (set the scene, pose a question, or share an anecdote)
  - Rising action (the technical challenge, failed attempts, or unexpected findings)
  - Climax (the breakthrough, solution, or key insight)
  - Resolution (what you learned, what’s next, or open questions)
- Keep paragraphs short and punchy but prefer running text over lists or bullets

### 3. Draft with Technical Depth
- Dive into the technical details: code, algorithms, data, and decisions.
- Use code snippets and visualizations to illustrate key points.
- Link to relevant resources, tools, or datasets (use URLs, not academic citations).
- Maintain a conversational, engaging tone—write as if explaining to a peer.

### 4. Add Visuals and Code
- Insert code blocks for important snippets (explain what/why, not just how).
- Add figures, charts, or screenshots to break up text and clarify concepts.
- Use Hugo’s shortcodes or Markdown for images and code (see Hugo docs).

### 5. Review for Engagement and Length
- Read aloud to check for flow and clarity.
- Ensure the post can be read in under 7 minutes (aim for ~1000-1400 words).
- Remove jargon unless it’s essential for technical accuracy.
- Make sure each section drives the story forward—cut anything that doesn’t serve the narrative.

### 6. Hugo-Specific Publishing Checklist
- Add or update front matter (title, date, tags, summary).
- Use Hugo’s image embedding and shortcode features for visuals.
- Check internal/external links.
- Preview the post locally with `hugo server`.
- Consult the [Hugo documentation](https://gohugo.io/documentation/) for advanced formatting, shortcodes, and troubleshooting.

---

## Completion Criteria
- Storyline is clear and engaging from start to finish.
- Technical depth is demonstrated with code and visuals.
- All links and images work in Hugo preview.
- Reading time is under 7 minutes.
- Conversational tone throughout.

---

## Example Prompts
- “Draft a blog post about my latest machine learning project using this workflow.”
- “Help me turn this notebook into a Hugo-ready, story-driven article.”
- “Checklist for publishing a technical post with Hugo.”

---

## Related Customizations
- Add a skill for automating figure generation and embedding in Hugo.
- Create a checklist for SEO optimization in Hugo posts.
